The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.

The bioactive constituents of Lomatium deliciosum include volatile oils, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lomatium deliciosum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, and gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and nausea.
